summary: A limit describes the value a function approaches as its input approaches some point — even if the function is undefined exactly there. Limits are the foundation of calculus: they define both the derivative and the behaviour of a graph near asymptotes and at infinity.
method: Try direct substitution first: if f(a) is defined and finite, that is the limit.
If you get 0/0, factor and cancel the common factor, then substitute again.
For a limit at infinity, compare the growth of the numerator and denominator (divide by the highest power).
A one-sided limit (from the left or right) can differ from the two-sided limit near an asymptote.

example.title: A limit that needs cancelling
steps: Now substitute x = 1: the limit is 1 + 1.
